mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.0%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving corporate earnings upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.0%, or the predictive Rank IC falls below 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising real estate starts signals synchronized expansion in goods and services demand, compressing supply chain bottlenecks and boosting equity risk premiums for positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in more than 60% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, a contraction in commercial and industrial lending directly restricts corporate capital expenditure, suppressing future earnings growth and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of observations during tightening periods despite declining credit conditions, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX levels.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP directly expands the corporate earnings base, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week as analysts update earnings estimates.
failure_condition: The rolling Spearman correlation between quarterly GDP growth and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.10 over any 5-year window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 2.5% year-over-year growth, persistent inflation erodes real consumer purchasing power and forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, depressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where CPI growth exceeds 2.5%, or the signal's predictive Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid employment growth coupled with declining housing starts indicates a structural shift in capital allocation away from real estate toward corporate expansion, driving sector rotation into cyclicals and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction between employment gains and housing declines fails to predict SPX direction in more than 55% of out-of-sample windows, or the joint signal's Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a stagflationary environment, stagnant industrial output paired with elevated price levels forces equity risk premiums higher, directly depressing valuation multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of observations during stagflationary periods despite the signal, or the predictive power vanishes when controlling for bond yield spreads.
